summary: This gene-environment association study examined 422 individuals and found that CRHR1 genetic polymorphisms moderated the effects of childhood abuse on adult depression. Multiple SNPs showed significant interactions (rs110402, P=0.008), with a protective TCA haplotype in intron 1 demonstrating significant protective effects (P<0.001). Findings replicated in an independent sample of 199 ethnically distinct women.
study_design: Gene-by-environment association study examining CRHR1 polymorphisms and childhood abuse interactions on adult depressive symptoms in 422 primary participants (97.4% African American, low SES) with replication in 199 women (87.7% Caucasian, middle-income)
